# Forever Here — QRHeadstone

> A QR memorial platform for people and pets. Families fill out a short form; their loved one's story is written in the first person — in that person's own voice. A QR code is created and can be placed on a headstone, ash vase, photo frame, or remembrance shrine. When someone scans it, they read the story as if the person themselves is speaking.

## What it does

- Families describe a loved one or pet through a guided form (personality, memories, relationships, favourite things)
- A 4–6 paragraph first-person memorial story is crafted from those memories, in English, Spanish, or both
- A QR code is created and linked permanently to the memorial page
- The QR can be printed on sticker paper or engraved and placed on any physical memorial object
- Visitors can leave memories and messages on the memorial page
- Family can add photos, music links, and legacy links (social media, published work, etc.)

## Key pages

- `/` — Landing page explaining the service
- `/create` — Three-step form to create a memorial
- `/remember/[id]` — Individual memorial page (public, scannable by anyone with the QR code)

## Supported languages

English and Spanish. Memorial stories can be written in either or both languages.

## Who uses it

- Families who have lost a loved one and want to create a lasting digital memorial
- Pet owners who want to honor a companion animal
- Funeral homes looking to offer a modern memorial service

## Technology

Built with TanStack Start, React, and Supabase (PostgreSQL + storage).

## Contact

Site: https://www.qrheadstone.com
Brand: Forever Here
